title: Verstappen And Red Bull’s Massive Problems Explained | F1 Explainer
description:
What has happened to Red Bull Racing and McLaren? The teams that once dominated Formula 1 now find themselves in deep trouble. In China, everything went wrong: Max Verstappen had issues and retired, Isack Hadjar spun, the RB22 lacks both speed and reliability, and McLaren did not even make it to the start. Meanwhile, Mercedes and Ferrari simply drove away from the field.

In this video, we analyse the major technical problems facing Red Bull and McLaren. From Max Verstappen’s disastrous weekend

description:
F1TV commentator and former F1 driver Jolyon Palmer joins Tom Clarkson in the Shanghai paddock to reflect on an action-packed Chinese Grand Prix.

Kimi Antonelli became the second youngest Grand Prix winner in F1 history, leading George Russell to the second consecutive Mercedes 1-2 of 2026. So can Kimi fight his more experienced teammate for the title this year?

description:
The newest team in Formula 1 are here to compete. Cadillac have earned the respect of their rivals. Now they’re pushing to get faster.

CEO Dan Towriss takes Tom Clarkson back to the start of the story - how he and his TWG Motorsports company first became involved in discussions about F1, conversations with General Motors and Cadillac, the work to build a team, choosing their race drivers and making their F1 debut in Australia.

Sergio Perez and Valtteri Bottas are the first Cadillac F1 drivers.

title: 2026 F1 Australian GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: The factory Mercedes team - George Russell heading Kimi Antonelli - duly finished one-two in Melbourne on Sunday, exhibiting more power, more downforce, more grip and more balance than all of their opposition - and capped all of that with perfect strategy calls. Ferrari pushed them hardest, with Charles Leclerc taking the lead into T1 and exchanging early-lap power surges with George Russell, but weird strategies quickly knocked them back to P3-P4, with Charles Leclerc crossing the line 0.6sec a

title: Australian grand prix Review | Day 10 of learning #F1 #drivetosurvive  #f1explained
description:
Ferrari… we have the car, we have the drivers… but strategy?”

What a crazy season opener at the Australian Grand Prix 🏁
George Russell wins the race, leading a Mercedes 1-2 with Kimi Antonelli, while Ferrari finishes P3 and P4 with Leclerc and Hamilton despite strong pace.

Meanwhile Max Verstappen climbed from P20 to P6, one of the drives of the race.

The 2026 F1 season has started with drama already.

title: What we've learned already at F1's Australian GP
description:
Make us your paddock pass for F1 2026! Join The Race Members' Club - get 90% off your first month here: https://www.patreon.com/cw/TheRace/membership

After a long pre-season, Formula 1's finally had its first proper day of 2026 at the Australian Grand Prix - which has given some big pointers for the season opener.

Ferrari and McLaren topped the two sessions - so pre-season favourite Mercedes was never fastest on Friday. So, we're in for a surprise, right?

00:00 Mercedes is actually ahead
01:1

title: 2026 F1 AUSTRALIAN GP Friday analysis by Peter Windsor
description: It was a big day in Melbourne for the local hero, Oscar Piastri, heading, as he did, the FP2 times in Melbourne ahead of the two factory Mercedes drivers (Kimi Antonelli and George Russell).  Showing all the original style qualities of  the driver who burst into F1 in 2023, Oscar looked right at home in the new McLaren MCL40-Mercedes, proving not only to be quick through the demanding T9-T10 section but also, simultaneously, over the lap as a whole. This being the first public exhibition of F1's

title: New F1 fans get LOST watching races... this guide explains EVERYTHING in 60 seconds
description:
New to F1? This will save you on race day 🏁

If you’re a new Formula One fan, race broadcasts can look overwhelming. So many numbers. So many abbreviations. So much happening at once.

Relax. It’s way simpler than it looks.

At the very top, you’ll see the total number of laps and how many have been completed. That tells you how far into the race you are.

On the left side of the screen is the leaderboard. Each driver’s current position is shown next to their name. If someone overtakes, you’ll

title: Formula 1 Cars, Explained with Max Verstappen
description:
These are more than cars. They’re science experiments.
If you enjoy this episode, subscribe to support more optimistic stories about tech!

I love Formula 1. This isn’t just a car race, it’s a massive group science competition. Ten teams are all fighting to be first, and on every team there are hundreds of people, spending millions of dollars, all working together to push technology to its limit. 

In this video, we’re gonna show you what it takes to build and drive some of the fastest and most
